intent: Aggregate SecurityRecipes secure-context, MCP, A2A, identity, telemetry, exposure, readiness, and evidence packs into a single enterprise posture snapshot that AI platform, security, procurement, and acquisition diligence teams can use to decide whether agentic AI systems are ready to operate, scale, or pause.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Security Architecture, Product Security, AppSec, IAM, GRC, procurement security, model-risk, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: Agentic AI posture management for secure context and MCP operations
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can show which agents exist, which identities and MCP namespaces they use, which context they retrieve, where XPIA and high-autonomy exposure concentrates, which workflows are scale-ready, and which evidence proves the current posture.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI has moved from assistant UX into tool-using and multi-agent systems. Enterprises need continuous posture evidence across context, tools, identity, handoffs, guardrails, and runtime traces before they can safely expand autonomous workflows.

decisions.meaning: The open reference posture has enough generated evidence, inventory, and runtime controls to support enterprise pilot and diligence conversations.
The posture is credible, but high-exposure paths, pilot connectors, or actionable poisoning findings require bounded rollout and owner review.
Missing or stale posture evidence prevents expansion until the source pack is regenerated or remediated.
A runtime event indicates secret exposure, prohibited egress, rogue authority, or an XPIA-sensitive high-autonomy path and the agent session should stop.

posture_dimensions.title: Agent and Component Inventory
posture_dimensions.question: Can the program enumerate workflows, agents, identities, MCP connectors, context sources, and evidence artifacts?

posture_dimensions.title: Identity and Delegated Authority
posture_dimensions.question: Are non-human identities, explicit denies, token rules, and MCP authorization decisions visible before agents act?

posture_dimensions.title: MCP Tool Surface Control
posture_dimensions.question: Are MCP namespaces registered, risk-tiered, authorization-bound, and checked for session-level exfiltration paths?

posture_dimensions.title: Context Integrity and Egress
posture_dimensions.question: Is retrieved context owned, hashed, demoted from instruction to evidence, scanned for poisoning, and bounded before egress?

posture_dimensions.title: Inter-Agent and Skill Boundary
posture_dimensions.question: Are A2A Agent Cards, handoffs, and agent skills treated as untrusted supply-chain inputs until controls are verified?

posture_dimensions.title: Runtime Guardrails and Telemetry
posture_dimensions.question: Can the program reconstruct agent runs with guardrail, tool-call, egress, approval, telemetry, and receipt evidence?

posture_dimensions.title: Exposure Path Management
posture_dimensions.question: Does the program rank the paths where context, identities, MCP tools, autonomy, and external destinations combine into material exposure?

posture_dimensions.title: Standards and Buyer Diligence
posture_dimensions.question: Can SecurityRecipes answer current OWASP, NIST, MCP, OpenAI, A2A, and procurement questions with generated evidence?

risk_factors.title: High Autonomy plus Indirect Prompt Injection
risk_factors.control_goal: Identify workflows where untrusted context can influence high-impact tool use, then require human approval, tool guardrails, and telemetry before action.

risk_factors.title: Private Data, Untrusted Content, and External Write in One Session
risk_factors.control_goal: Block or split sessions where tool combinations create a path from sensitive context to ticket, webhook, registry, model, or public egress.

risk_factors.title: Agent Skill, Agent Card, or Tool Description Drift
risk_factors.control_goal: Pin, hash, diff-review, and rescan skills, Agent Cards, tool schemas, and MCP server metadata before promotion.

risk_factors.title: Identity and Privilege Abuse
risk_factors.control_goal: Require per-workflow non-human identities, short-lived tokens, audience binding, explicit denies, and runtime revocation.

risk_factors.title: Context Poisoning or Stale Secure Context
risk_factors.control_goal: Fail closed when registered context has actionable poisoning findings, stale hashes, or missing owner recertification.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
standards_alignment.coverage: Agent goal hijack, tool misuse, identity and privilege abuse, agentic supply chain, unexpected code execution, memory and context poisoning, insecure inter-agent communication, cascading failures, human-agent trust exploitation, and rogue agents.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Agentic Skills Top 10
standards_alignment.coverage: Skill registry compromise, over-privileged skills, weak isolation, update drift, insecure metadata, missing governance, scanning gaps, and incident response for agent execution layers.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
standards_alignment.coverage: OAuth 2.1 resource-server expectations for HTTP MCP transports, resource indicators, audience binding, PKCE, token handling, and confused-deputy controls.

standards_alignment.name: OpenAI Agents SDK Guardrails
standards_alignment.coverage: Input, output, and tool guardrails, tripwires, workflow boundaries, hosted-tool limits, and tool-call validation before and after execution.

standards_alignment.name: Agent2Agent Protocol
standards_alignment.coverage: Agent discovery, Agent Cards, JSON-RPC over HTTP(S), streaming, push notifications, authentication, observability, and secure interoperability between opaque agents.

standards_alignment.name: Microsoft Security - A new era of agents, a new era of posture
standards_alignment.coverage: Agent posture, indirect prompt injection risk, high-autonomy risk factors, coordinator agents, attack-path analysis, and hardening guidance for reducing agent attack surface.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
standards_alignment.coverage: Generative AI governance, mapping, measurement, third-party dependencies, data provenance, monitoring, incident response, and lifecycle risk treatment.

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: SecurityRecipes now produces a posture snapshot for the secure context layer, showing whether agentic workflows have enough inventory, identity, MCP, context, handoff, telemetry, exposure, and standards evidence to expand.
default_questions_answered: Which agentic workflows can scale, pilot, or pause?
Which agents and identities can reach which MCP namespaces?
Where do high-autonomy, XPIA-sensitive, or exfiltration-capable paths concentrate?
Which source packs prove the posture decision?
Which gaps should an enterprise buyer or acquirer prioritize first?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Attach the posture snapshot to AI platform quarterly posture review, procurement security review, MCP gateway promotion, model-agent launch review, and acquisition diligence.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with the open posture snapshot, then sell hosted posture diffs, customer runtime evidence ingestion, private MCP exposure graphs, executive risk dashboards, and trust-center APIs.
